Finanace Simulator

Monte Carlo analysis of returns, inflation, and dynamic flows.

Sequence of Returns Planning

Maximize realistic withdrawals within your risk tolerance. The focus is investment drawdown tradeoffs - accounts, budgets, RMDs, pensions, Social Security, real estate, etc. are out of scope. Everything in this tool — portfolio balances, withdrawals, and market returns — uses real (today’s) dollars, so you don’t need to consider inflation. Market returns and inflation are based on historical data.

Use a range of years around the endpoint. Leave both at 0 for a fixed timeline.

000s
ConservativeBalancedAggressive

Total Lifetime Early Withdrawal

Optionally rank success by early-weighted spending. Does not change boosts or guardrails — set those in Withdrawal Strategy for a front-loaded plan.

Find Best Plan

Search for the highest sustainable spending schedule. Check Include in search on settings below to choose what to tune.

Minimum chance of never running out and leaving at least the Target Ending Balance.

Depth of dynamic ajustments and allowed spending shortfall vs. plan - 0% = stay on plan; higher = bigger plan, more belt-tightening in bad markets.

000s

Balance to leave behind at the end of the horizon, on top of never running out.

Adjust allocation over time

Asset Allocation above is year 0. Each tier below is a later target mix — the plan glides linearly between them. Intermediate Years is how long the glide to that tier takes; the last tier covers remaining years. Keep one tier that matches your starting mix for a flat allocation. Each tier’s categories must sum to 100%.

Fees & Taxes

Optional rough estimation of fee and tax impact to plan. Off by default assumes plan withdrawals already have tax and fee payments baked in. When you add taxes, the plan withdrawals convert to net of taxes.

%

Percent of portfolio each year (advisor fee and/or fund expenses). Applied after market growth, even in down years.

Withdrawal tax over time

Stage an effective tax on spending you withdraw. Amounts in Withdrawal Strategy are what you spend or receive; the portfolio withdraws more to cover tax (tax ≈ rate × net). Glide surplus is always taxed. Gifts are taxed only when the tier checkbox is on (e.g. gifts to children). Major events are not taxed here. Optional nested spending levels apply higher rates only to net spending above each threshold.

Withdrawal Strategy
000s

In today's dollars — the simulator keeps purchasing power constant, so don't build in inflation raises. When Fees & Taxes withdrawal-tax tiers are set, this is what you spend; the portfolio withdraws more to cover tax.

Spending Over Time

Use tiers to shape spending with extra withdrawal (adds to base) and annual change (negative = spend more now, less later). When Find Best Plan includes this section, it tunes only the first tier's extra withdrawal.

Major Events

Model expected cash events outside your spending schedule — selling a house or business, inheritance, reverse-mortgage draws, or known large payments. Positive amounts add to the portfolio; negative amounts are extra payments on top of that year's plan. Start year is year 1 of retirement. Leave Years blank for a one-time event, or enter how many consecutive years it repeats. Not used with a Specific List — bake those into your list instead. Events appear as dots on the schedule chart below.

Minimum withdrawal

Limits how far guardrails can cut your plan each year (capped by portfolio balance) — your schedule above is the plan; the chart below shows the plan line and this floor separately. Add tiers to stage different minimums over time — the last tier applies to all remaining years. Remove all tiers for no minimum. Doesn't apply to a Specific List.

Years in a row at the minimum before returning to plan. 0 = off.

yrs

Spend at least the plan for this many years after the streak. 0 = off.

Paste from a spreadsheet — new lines, tabs, commas, semicolons, or pipes. Negative values are deposits. Last value is reused for remaining years. When Fees & Taxes withdrawal-tax tiers are set, amounts are what you spend; the portfolio withdraws more to cover tax.

Minimum withdrawal

Limits how far market/balance adjustments can cut each year's list amount (capped by portfolio balance) — the list above is the plan. Add tiers to stage different minimums over time — the last tier applies to all remaining years. Remove all tiers for no minimum.

Years in a row at the minimum before returning to plan. 0 = off.

yrs

Spend at least the plan for this many years after the streak. 0 = off.

Gifting

Add extra gift withdrawals in years when spending is above the trigger adjustment from plan. Gifts pay only when the portfolio (after growth and your regular withdrawal or deposit) exceeds Balance >. The gift amount scales from 0% at the trigger to 100% at the target. Leave both blank to have the gift pay 100% if plan and balance are exceeded. Add tiers to shape giving over time, e.g., use a -100% target and trigger to ensure gifts for a period of time.

Dynamic Adjustments & Guardrails

Market adjustment

Adjust withdrawals based on real market return. At the Expected return the plan is on track, so its adjustment is normally 0. Downward adjustments are skipped when the balance is above No cut. Upward adjustments cannot exceed the Max boost drawdown of remaining balance.

000s
blank = off
%
blank = off

Expected
%
000s

%
000s
Balance adjustment

Scale total withdrawal based on portfolio balance: below the floor, spending ramps down toward the max cut; above the ceiling, it ramps up by the boost rate for every extra multiple of the ceiling — with no upper cap. Between the two, spending is unchanged.

Floor Balance Max Cut
000s
%

0 = no floor

Ceiling Balance Boost Rate
000s
%

0 = no ceiling

Glide-path spend-down

Actively spend surplus down toward a target ending balance. Each year the simulator computes the balance still needed to fund your remaining plan and land on the end target. When the portfolio still sits above that glide path, the spend rate share of the remaining surplus is withdrawn. Slide spend timing toward later to hold surplus back for late retirement.

000s

blank = off; 0 = spend to zero

%

share of surplus spent each year

later balanced sooner

    Explore specific paths

    Withdrawal Heatmap

    View withdrawal patterns across time for all simulations. You can analyze static snapshots or animate the display to see patterns in withdrawals. Color modes compare withdrawals vs. your plan, vs. the classic flat 4% rule, vs. the median for each year, or show absolute withdrawal amounts. There is also control to visually emphasize the early years.

    Sequence Risk: IRR vs Average Return

    Each dot is one simulation. Dots below the dashed diagonal were hurt by early bad years — their money-weighted IRR trails their time-weighted average return; dots above it got lucky early. Paths in the lower-right had good average returns but still fell short of the plan: sequence risk in its purest form. Click a dot to see that path's withdrawals against its plan.

    Average Timelines

    Shows portfolio balance and dynamic withdrawals over time for the 65th through 5th percentile paths. Point size and shape reflect each year's real return.

    Distribution of Real Returns

    Summary of average annual real return across all simulations. Each stat also shows the IRR — the money-weighted return, which accounts for the timing of withdrawals and deposits.

    Distribution of IRR

    Money-weighted real return across all simulations — the same distribution view as the average return above, but weighted by when money was actually in the portfolio.

    Median IRR
    Required IRR

    Running simulations, calculating paths...

    Advanced simulation settings

    How many CPU cores to use when running simulations. Higher is faster but uses more of your computer while the run is in progress.

    %

    Averages a band of runs around each percentile (P5–P65) to reduce noise. 0 = a single run.

    Primary metric for ranking (P5–P65), on-plan success, 3D coloring, and Find Best Plan. Auto picks mean/yr (total ÷ years lived) when a horizon range is set. Total withdrawn is still shown alongside results. When the Withdrawals slider favors Early Withdrawal, early-weighted spending overrides median/yr for ranking.

    Max spending shortfall vs. plan when scoring runs. Affects the “on plan” success rate, blue below-plan markers, and 3D chart coloring — including after Find Best Plan. Find Best Plan’s own Risk Tolerance only shapes the search.

    Used when the Withdrawals slider is not on Total Lifetime. Early emphasis steepens the drop after year 1; Late floor keeps the last years counting (never a hard zero).

    Adds year-to-year persistence by drawing runs of consecutive years averaging this length (Historical Resampling and Smoothed Historical); set to 1 for fully independent years. Log-Normal uses it for year-to-year smoothing instead.

    Softens Smoothed Historical's output so it isn't limited to only the exact years in your selected range. Not used by the other two methods.

    How many simulations Find Best Plan runs to score each candidate plan while searching. Lower = faster search but noisier results; higher = slower but more reliable. The final plan is always confirmed using the full Number of Simulations above.
